stories.title: Bound auto-updater network calls with a 30-second timeout
Emit SHA256 sidecars for .deb, .rpm, and .AppImage release artifacts
Purge Cloudflare cache after R2 repo sync
stories.review_note: CLOSED (v0.2.1 US-010): original CONSIDER about a cf-cache-status smoke assertion is addressed by prd-v0.2.1-release-hardening US-002 (regression guard on binary-<arch>/Packages in InRelease) + US-004 (post-publish end-to-end apt/dnf install smoke). Both land in v0.2.1. Primary purge + retry-with-backoff remain correctly implemented.

stories.title: Pin CI workflow base image to ubuntu-22.04
Pin appimageupdatetool to a versioned tag with SHA256 verification
Clear stale review notes from the Linux packaging migration status file
Gate the legacy .run installer code path with #[cfg(unix)]
Wire macOS .dmg update install flow into self-update dispatch
stories.review_note: CLOSED (v0.2.1 US-010): Deferred to v0.3.0 macOS release cut — `cp -R` is adequate for the current PaneFlow.app layout (no nested frameworks with resource forks). Reopen if bundle gains Swift dylibs or embedded frameworks. Hardware smoke-test (hdiutil + /Applications replace) is tracked in prd-macos-port.md and remains a v0.3.0 gate.

stories.title: Wire Windows .msi update install flow into self-update dispatch
stories.review_note: CLOSED (v0.2.1 US-010): Deferred to v0.3.0 Windows release cut — requires hardware-validated test case. Track as WIN-001 in the v0.3.0 port PRD (prd-windows-port.md). Hardware smoke-test (msiexec UAC + exit codes) remains a v0.3.0 gate; the msiexec 1618 'another install in progress' arm will be added alongside the hardware smoke test that can actually exercise it.

stories.title: Dispatch xdg-open / open / explorer.exe per-OS in reveal-in-file-manager sites
stories.review_note: CLOSED (v0.2.1 US-010): both the SHOULD_FIX (workspace_ops/mod.rs two-argv explorer bug) AND the CONSIDER (sidebar/mod.rs open::that comment) addressed by prd-v0.2.1-release-hardening US-007. The Rust source is verifiable: OsString flag + push(path.as_os_str()) single-token pattern at workspace_ops/mod.rs, and the sidebar comment now accurately describes ShellExecuteW vs `cmd /C start`. Runtime validation on a Windows 11 host is operator-gated and belongs to the v0.3.0 Windows release cut.
